extraction.quote: Our consolidated gross margin percentage increased to 74% for the second quarter of 2026 from 56% for the first quarter of 2026

validation.rule: Each metric must appear in ≥2 distinct locations OR have one high-confidence source (income statement). Material conflicts MUST be flagged.
validation.detail: All five metrics (Revenue, Net income, EPS · diluted, EPS · basic, Gross margin) are corroborated across ≥2 distinct locations with no material conflicts. All figures are internally consistent within rounding tolerances. No GAAP vs. non-GAAP conflicts detected — all EPS sources are labelled GAAP.

per_metric.reason: Identical value ($23,860M) confirmed in 2 distinct locations: Consolidated Statements of Operations (p.10, primary income statement) and MD&A · Results of Operations (p.28). Both verified. Zero discrepancy.

Identical value ($13,785M) confirmed in 4 distinct locations: Consolidated Statements of Operations (p.10, verified), Consolidated Statements of Comprehensive Income (p.11, unverified but consistent), MD&A · Results of Operations (p.28, verified), and EPS note GAAP (p.24, verified). Strong multi-source corroboration; the single unverified instance does not affect confidence given the three verified agreements.

Identical GAAP diluted EPS ($12.07) appears in 2 locations: Consolidated Statements of Operations (p.10, GAAP, verified) and EPS note (p.24, GAAP, unverified but consistent). Both sources are GAAP-labelled; no non-GAAP figure present. The p.10 source is a high-confidence income statement, and p.24 corroborates exactly. Confidence high despite p.24 being unverified due to primary income statement presence.

Identical GAAP basic EPS ($12.25) appears in 2 locations: Consolidated Statements of Operations (p.10, GAAP, verified) and EPS note (p.24, GAAP, unverified but consistent). Same rationale as diluted EPS — primary income statement plus corroborating note. No non-GAAP counterpart present.

Gross margin of 74% (raw 74.4%) confirmed in 2 distinct locations: MD&A · Results of Operations (p.28, verified) and MD&A · Gross margin commentary (p.29, verified). The two sources agree within 0.0% (both state 74%), well within the 0.1% tolerance threshold. No conflict.

narrative: Revenue, net income, and EPS data are provided only for the current quarter, with no prior-quarter or prior-year figures supplied, making period-over-period comparison impossible with the given data. Current-quarter gross margin stands at 74%, with diluted EPS of $12.07 on net income of $13.79B against revenue of $23.86B. To generate the required numeric comparisons, please provide at least one additional period's figures.

confidence.summary: The filing data itself is exceptionally clean — 5/5 high-confidence corroborations, no conflicts, and GAAP-only EPS (eps_gap: null) — anchoring confidence firmly upward; however, a massive net insider sell of ~$14.4 B across 49 transactions in 90 days (vs. 1 buy) introduces a significant contradictory signal that meaningfully drags the score down, and price-trend data is too thin to provide useful alignment information.

factors.name: Inter-document agreement
factors.signal: All 5 metrics corroborated across 6 distinct sources; validation passed with zero conflicts; eps_gap is null (GAAP-only EPS).
factors.detail: conf_tally is 5 high / 0 med / 0 low across 5 metrics drawn from 6 distinct sources. validation_passed=true, validation_conflict=false. eps_gap=null means the filing reports only GAAP EPS with no non-GAAP reconciliation needed — the cleanest possible configuration per EPS-gap rules, contributing positively. No arithmetic conflicts detected anywhere in the 10-Q.

factors.name: Cross-source consistency
factors.signal: Only 1 period available (EPS: $12.07); no sign reversals or low-confidence periods, but single-period history limits trend verification.
factors.detail: periods_available=1 with eps_series=[12.07]. eps_sign_reversals=0 and periods_with_low_conf_metric=0 are both clean signals, but with only one historical data point there is no multi-period trend to validate continuity against. This caps the positive contribution of this factor — we cannot confirm the data is continuous across quarters.

factors.name: Insider activity & news
factors.signal: 49 insider sells vs. 1 buy; net insider value = −$14,423,267,404. Headlines are broadly bullish (AI partnership, stock surge, price targets), creating a mixed picture.
factors.detail: insider_net_value_usd of −$14.42 B over 90 days from 49 sell transactions against just 1 buy is an extremely large negative insider signal — one of the largest possible divergences between insider behavior and reported fundamentals. Headlines tell a different story: 'Micron surges 5.5% on blockbuster Anthropic AI deal,' 'Prediction: Micron Stock Will Reach $1,200,' and new partnership announcements are all bullish. The stark contradiction between insider disposition at scale and positive news flow introduces material uncertainty about the reliability of the forward narrative reflected in filings and press.

factors.name: Price-trend alignment
factors.signal: Price data is insufficient: only 2 data points, 0 coverage days, no 30d/90d pct_change available. Factor is heavily down-weighted.
factors.detail: data_points=2, coverage_days=0, pct_change_30d=null, pct_change_90d=null. While price_slope_sign=1 (positive) exists, aligns_with_price=null and earnings_direction=0 mean no meaningful alignment check can be performed. This factor is down-weighted to 0.12 (from a typical ~0.20) due to the near-total absence of price coverage. The positive slope sign is noted but carries negligible evidentiary weight given the data thinness.
